proposed_hypotheses.text: Cetuximab improves pfs_months specifically in patients who are wild-type for KRAS, NRAS, and BRAF V600E (RAS/RAF wild-type), with a positive cetuximab x RAS/RAF-WT interaction.
Pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in patients with msi_high=1, with a positive pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction.
Encorafenib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with braf_v600e=1, with a positive encorafenib x braf_v600e interaction.
Trastuzumab/tucatinib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with her2_amplified=1, with a positive trastuzumab_tucatinib x her2_amplified interaction.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind in ras_raf_wt strata; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_cetuximab*ras_raf_wt', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: In RAS/RAF wild-type patients (n=25318), cetuximab+ mean PFS 4.444 vs cetuximab- 4.489 (diff=-0.045, p=0.174). In RAS/RAF mutated, diff=-0.034 (p=0.252). Interaction term coef=-0.012, p=0.792. Cetuximab confers no benefit even in the canonical anti-EGFR-eligible subgroup; H1 is refuted.
In MSI-high (n=2513): pembro+ 4.299 vs pembro- 4.292 (diff=0.007, p=0.955). In MSI-stable: diff=0.005, p=0.861. Interaction coef=0.002, p=0.988. H2 is refuted.

In BRAF V600E patients (n=2272): encorafenib+ 3.979 vs encorafenib- 4.108 (diff=-0.129, p=0.359). Interaction coef=-0.141, p=0.376. Encorafenib does NOT improve PFS in BRAF V600E; H3 is refuted (point estimate is in the wrong direction).
In HER2-amplified (n=1504): trast/tucatinib+ 4.273 vs trast/tucatinib- 4.256 (diff=0.017, p=0.939). Interaction coef=0.063, p=0.778. H4 is refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
stage_iv=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 (negative effect).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ecog_ps + age_years + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ecog_ps coefficient = -1.179 months per ECOG point (p<1e-300). H5 supported with very strong magnitude.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ stage_iv + age_years + ecog_ps + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted stage_iv coefficient = -1.358 months (p<1e-300). H6 supported with very strong magnitude.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ albumin_g_dl + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted albumin coefficient = +0.462 months per g/dL (p<1e-300). H7 supported.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ weight_loss_pct_6mo + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted weight_loss_pct_6mo coefficient = -0.076 months per percentage point lost (p<1e-300). H8 supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: right_sided_primary=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided primaries (negative effect).
kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect).
braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect).
nras_mutation=1 is associated with longer pfs_months (positive effect, contrary to typical clinical expectation).

analyses.result_summary: Univariate right_sided_primary coefficient = -0.305 months (p=5.2e-46). In multivariable model (adjusted), coefficient = -0.287 (p<1e-300). H9 supported.
Univariate kras_mutation coefficient = -0.327 months (p=8.5e-56). Multivariable coefficient = -0.354 (p<1e-300). H10 supported.
Univariate braf_v600e coefficient = -0.228 months (p=3.7e-06). Multivariable coefficient = -0.356 (p<1e-50). H11 supported.

Univariate nras_mutation coefficient = +0.221 months (p=0.00023). After adjustment for KRAS, BRAF, ECOG, stage etc, the effect attenuates to +0.021 (p=0.367). Univariate H12 supported, but the signal disappears with adjustment, suggesting confounding (e.g., NRAS-mutated patients tend to be RAS/RAF WT for KRAS+BRAF).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope), opposite to the usual aging-as-poor-prognosis expectation.
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age_years coefficient = +0.176 months per year of age (p<1e-300). The positive direction is robust to adjustment for ECOG, stage, albumin, and weight loss. H13 supported.
Univariate cea coefficient = -0.0053 per ng/mL (p=3.1e-17). Multivariable coefficient = -0.0050 (p<1e-50). H14 supported.
Univariate ldh coefficient = -0.00029 per U/L (p=0.013). Multivariable coefficient = -0.00042 (p<1e-15). H15 supported with small magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib has a positive main effect on pfs_months (longer PFS in regorafenib-treated patients) after adjustment for prognostic covariates.
treatment_cetuximab has no main effect on pfs_months.
treatment_bevacizumab has no main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + sex_female + ecog_ps + stage_iv + right_sided_primary + kras_mutation + nras_mutation + braf_v600e + msi_high + her2_amplified + cea_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + weight_loss_pct_6mo + crp_mg_l + nlr + treatment_cetuximab + treatment_bevacizumab + treatment_pembrolizumab + treatment_encorafenib + treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib + treatment_regorafenib + liver_mets + bone_mets + prior_lines_of_therapy + years_since_diagnosis', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment coefficients: regorafenib +0.943 (p<1e-300, R^2=0.86); cetuximab -0.011 (p=0.187); bevacizumab +0.003 (p=0.739); pembrolizumab +0.0001 (p=0.996); encorafenib +0.006 (p=0.633); trastuzumab/tucatinib +0.026 (p=0.068). H16 strongly supported. H17 and H18 supported as null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_regorafenib is concentrated in patients with kras_mutation=0 (a negative regorafenib x kras_mutation interaction term).
The benefit of treatment_regorafenib is concentrated in patients with braf_v600e=0 (a negative regorafenib x braf_v600e interaction term).
The benefit of treatment_regorafenib is concentrated in patients with right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided), with a negative regorafenib x right_sided_primary interaction.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified: KRAS-WT regorafenib effect +1.656 months (p≈0), KRAS-mutant +0.027 (p=0.45). Adjusted interaction term regorafenib x kras_mutation = -1.633 (p<1e-300). H19 strongly supported.
Stratified: BRAF-WT regorafenib effect +1.027 months (p≈0), BRAF-V600E -0.156 (p=0.147). Adjusted interaction term regorafenib x braf_v600e = -0.990 (p≈5.3e-104). H20 strongly supported.
Stratified: left-sided regorafenib effect +1.480 months (p≈0); right-sided +0.026 (p=0.52). Adjusted interaction term regorafenib x right_sided_primary = -1.463 (p<1e-300). H21 strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with right_sided_primary=0 AND ras_raf_wt=1 (left-sided RAS/RAF wild-type), treatment_regorafenib provides a substantially larger PFS benefit than in any other side x molecular subgroup, reflected in a positive 3-way interaction regorafenib x left_sided x ras_raf_wt.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib*left_sided*ras_raf_wt + ecog_ps + stage_iv + age_years + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Stratified mean PFS differences (regorafenib+ vs regorafenib-): left/WT +2.749 (p≈0), left/mut +0.209 (p=6e-7), right/WT +0.071 (p=0.20), right/mut -0.021 (p=0.71). Adjusted 3-way interaction coefficient = +2.550 (p<1e-300). Within left-sided RAS/RAF-WT regorafenib-treated patients (n=3264), mean PFS = 6.866 months vs ~4.12 elsewhere. H22 strongly supported. R^2 of 3-way model = 0.906.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the left-sided RAS/RAF wild-type subgroup, only treatment_regorafenib confers a meaningful PFS benefit; cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib all have null effects.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ all_treatments + ecog_ps + stage_iv + age_years + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df[(left_sided==1)&(ras_raf_wt==1)])
analyses.result_summary: Within left-sided RAS/RAF-WT (n=16443): regorafenib coef +2.727 (p<1e-300); cetuximab -0.031 (p=0.097); bevacizumab +0.015 (p=0.40); pembrolizumab +0.021 (p=0.38); encorafenib +0.003 (p=0.91); trast/tucatinib +0.082 (p=0.012, marginal small). H23 supported: regorafenib is the only treatment with a clinically meaningful effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Bevacizumab effect is modified by msi_high status (interaction).
Encorafenib effect is modified by nras_mutation (interaction).
Trastuzumab/tucatinib effect is modified by kras_mutation (interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction bevacizumab x msi_high = +0.069, p=0.077. Marginal at best; not robust. H24 weakly supported / inconclusive.
Adjusted interaction encorafenib x nras_mutation = -0.146, p=0.080. Marginal; not robust. H25 weakly supported / inconclusive.
Adjusted interaction trastuzumab_tucatinib x kras_mutation = -0.055, p=0.077. Marginal; not robust. H26 weakly supported / inconclusive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: race_ethnicity is associated with differences in pfs_months across categories (omnibus test).
insurance_type is associated with differences in pfs_months across categories.
rural_residence=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than urban residence.
sex_female=1 is associated with different pfs_months than sex_female=0 after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Group means: white 4.316, hispanic 4.315, asian 4.306, black 4.273, other 4.407. No category coefficient is significant; omnibus null. H27 refuted.
Group means: medicaid 4.277, medicare 4.312, private 4.330, uninsured 4.249. No coefficient is significant at p<0.05; omnibus null. H28 refuted.
rural_residence coefficient = +0.025 (p=0.34); point estimate is in opposite direction to hypothesis. H29 refuted.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ sex_female + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + kras_mutation + braf_v600e + right_sided_primary', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted sex_female coefficient = +0.013 (p=0.14). H30 refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, or other major comorbidities are associated with shorter pfs_months as univariate effects.

analyses.code: Univariate smf.ols for each comorbidity vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: All 15 comorbidity coefficients are non-significant (p>0.05) except depression_anxiety_diagnosis (-0.052, p=0.053, borderline). No reproducible signal that comorbidities meaningfully alter PFS in this cohort. H31 refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among lab values, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, hemoglobin_g_dl, calcium_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl have prognostic associations with pfs_months.

analyses.code: Univariate smf.ols for each lab value
analyses.result_summary: Univariate associations: alkaline_phosphatase coef -0.0006 (p=0.004), total_bilirubin -0.065 (p=0.044), hemoglobin -0.010 (p=0.078), calcium -0.033 (p=0.11), creatinine -0.046 (p=0.18). Only alkaline phosphatase is robust; bilirubin marginal; hemoglobin/calcium/creatinine null. H32 partially supported (alk phos only).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Common pharmacogenomic / disease-modifying SNPs (snp_rs1801131, snp_rs1050828, snp_rs1801197) show univariate associations with pfs_months.
After adjusting for major prognostic factors and treatment_regorafenib, no SNP shows a reproducible association with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate hits at p<0.05: snp_rs1801131 coef -0.049 (p=0.027), snp_rs1050828 +0.092 (p=0.015), snp_rs1801197 -0.049 (p=0.047). 3 of 28 SNPs significant — close to chance expectation under multiple testing. H33 modestly supported by counts; effect sizes are small.

analyses.code: smf.ols(pfs_months ~ snp + ecog_ps + stage_iv + age_years + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + kras_mutation + braf_v600e + right_sided_primary + treatment_regorafenib, data=df) for each SNP
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted SNP screen: snp_rs1045642 coef -0.016 (p=0.043), snp_rs1800629 +0.029 (p=0.005), snp_rs2070744 -0.017 (p=0.033). The univariate hits do not reproduce after adjustment; the adjusted hits are different SNPs and effect sizes are tiny (|coef|<0.03). Consistent with chance under multiple testing. H34 supported: no SNP shows a robust, large association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: liver_mets=1, bone_mets=1, adrenal_mets=1, pleural_effusion=1, and pericardial_effusion=1 each predict shorter pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ liver_mets + bone_mets + adrenal_mets + pleural_effusion + pericardial_effusion + ecog_ps + stage_iv + age_years + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted: liver_mets -0.012 (p=0.33); bone_mets +0.024 (p=0.030, opposite direction); adrenal_mets -0.004 (p=0.81); pleural_effusion -0.015 (p=0.22); pericardial_effusion -0.047 (p=0.033). Only pericardial effusion is in the hypothesized direction and significant; bone mets is significant but in the opposite direction. H35 largely refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps amplifies the negative effect of stage_iv on pfs_months (negative ecog_ps x stage_iv interaction).
Albumin_g_dl modifies the deleterious effect of weight_loss_pct_6mo (positive interaction: high albumin partially offsets weight loss harm).
Age_years modifies the regorafenib benefit (regorafenib effect varies with age).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ stage_iv*ecog_ps + age_years + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: stage_iv x ecog_ps interaction = +0.051 (p<1e-4). Direction is OPPOSITE to hypothesis (the harm of stage_iv is slightly attenuated at higher ECOG, perhaps because both already capture severe disease). H36 refuted in direction.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ albumin_g_dl*weight_loss_pct_6mo + ecog_ps + stage_iv + age_years', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term albumin x weight_loss = -0.0011 (p=0.63). H37 refuted; albumin and weight loss act additively, not synergistically.
Interaction age_years x regorafenib = +0.0012 (p=0.28). H38 refuted; regorafenib benefit is constant across age.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_regorafenib effect is attenuated at higher ecog_ps (negative regorafenib x ecog_ps interaction).
Treatment_bevacizumab effect is modified by right_sided_primary status.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction regorafenib x ecog_ps = -0.051 (p=0.00036). Small but significant: regorafenib benefit declines about 0.05 months per ECOG point. H39 supported with modest magnitude.
Adjusted interaction bev x right_sided_primary = -0.008 (p=0.65). H40 refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: msi_high=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than msi_low/stable.
her2_amplified=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than her2_amplified=0.
ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with different pfs_months than ntrk_fusion=0.

analyses.result_summary: msi_high coef = -0.019 (p=0.68). Multivariable coef = -0.008 (p=0.64). H41 refuted.
her2_amplified coef = -0.056 (p=0.35). H42 refuted.
ntrk_fusion coef = +0.040 (p=0.78). N=251 carriers; underpowered and null. H43 refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Years_since_diagnosis correlates positively with pfs_months (longer disease duration = stable disease).
Higher pain_nrs and fatigue_grade are associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: prior_lines_of_therapy coef = +0.008 (p=0.39); adjusted +0.001 (p=0.80). H44 refuted.
years_since_diagnosis coef = +0.009 (p=0.14); adjusted +0.003 (p=0.18). H45 refuted.
fatigue_grade coef = -0.002 (p=0.85); pain_nrs coef = -0.002 (p=0.55). H46 refuted; symptom grades are not associated with PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Smoking_pack_years is positively associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Education_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ smoking_pack_years', data=df); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ education_years', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: smoking_pack_years coef = +0.000004 (p=0.91); education_years coef = +0.003 (p=0.34). Both null. H47 and H48 refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within left-sided RAS/RAF wild-type patients, the regorafenib benefit is preserved across stage (stage_iv strata) and ECOG strata.

analyses.code: Within left-sided RAS/RAF WT subset, regorafenib effect by stage and ECOG strata via smf.ols
analyses.result_summary: Within left-sided RAS/RAF-WT (n=16443): regorafenib coefficient = +2.727 holding stage and ECOG fixed (p<1e-300). Stratified: regorafenib effect is positive in both stage_iv=0 and stage_iv=1 and across ECOG values (small ecog interaction = -0.05 per point as in H39). H49 supported: benefit is robust within this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib has a positive interaction with nras_mutation (regorafenib helps NRAS-mutated patients).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified: NRAS-WT regorafenib effect +0.941 (p≈3e-292), NRAS-mutant +1.929 (p≈1.5e-35). Adjusted regorafenib x nras_mutation interaction = +0.889 (p≈1.5e-57). H50 supported. NRAS mutants have unusually large regorafenib benefit. However, this is largely explained by nearly all NRAS-mutated cases also being KRAS-WT and BRAF-WT (i.e., they are RAS_KRAS-WT) — so the effect maps onto the broader 'KRAS-WT and BRAF-WT' regorafenib-responsive class.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher crp_mg_l (inflammation marker) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate crp coef = -0.0020 (p=0.065); adjusted coef = -0.0008 (p=0.055). Marginal; not significant. H51 refuted (or weakly suggestive).
Univariate nlr coef = +0.005 (p=0.29); adjusted +0.003 (p=0.084). H52 refuted (point estimate is in opposite direction to hypothesis and not significant).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among prior therapies (prior_chemotherapy, prior_radiation, prior_surgery, prior_immunotherapy, prior_targeted_therapy), at least one is associated with pfs_months in univariate analysis.

analyses.code: smf.ols for each prior therapy variable
analyses.result_summary: All five prior-therapy indicators have null univariate associations with PFS (all p>0.27, |coef|<0.03). H53 refuted. Prior treatment history does not predict subsequent PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib effect is preserved across patient sex (no sex interaction).
treatment_regorafenib effect varies with age (regorafenib x age_years interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted regorafenib x sex_female interaction is small and non-significant (estimated <0.05 magnitude, p>0.2 in exploratory check). H54 supported (no sex modification).
Interaction term = +0.0012 per year-of-age (p=0.28). H55 refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Considered jointly, pfs_months in this cohort is largely explained by a small set of clinical / molecular / treatment features: age_years (positive), ecog_ps (negative), stage_iv (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), kras_mutation (negative), braf_v600e (negative), right_sided_primary (negative), and treatment_regorafenib in left-sided RAS/RAF wild-type patients (large positive). Most other features (other treatments, MSI, HER2, NTRK, PIK3CA, TP53, comorbidities, demographics, SNPs) have null effects.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + right_sided_primary + kras_mutation + nras_mutation + braf_v600e + msi_high + her2_amplified + cea_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + weight_loss_pct_6mo + treatment_cetuximab + treatment_bevacizumab + treatment_pembrolizumab + treatment_encorafenib + treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib + treatment_regorafenib + treatment_regorafenib:left_sided:ras_raf_wt + ...', data=df)
analyses.result_summary: Three-way model R^2 = 0.906 — the listed features explain ~90% of pfs_months variance. The 3-way interaction regorafenib x left_sided x ras_raf_wt = +2.55 (p<1e-300) dominates the treatment landscape; biomarker-targeted classical agents (cetuximab in RAS-WT, pembro in MSI-H, encorafenib in BRAF V600E, trast/tucatinib in HER2-amp) all show null effects. H56 supported.
